dp:cite_as: Badreldin, N. & Youssef, A. (2026). Digital Pedon: A Digital Twin Framework for Soil Profile Monitoring, Physics-Based Inference, and Global Interoperability. Version 2.0. CC BY 4.0.
dp:description: Digital Pedon (DP) is a dictionary-first, stdlib-only Python framework that implements a three-layer digital twin for soil profiles. It ingests sensor and remote sensing data, runs physics-based solvers (Van Genuchten, Darcy-Buckingham, heat equation, Q10 respiration), fires threshold events, and stores a time-series history — all operating on plain Python dicts with no external dependencies.

dp:layer_structural.dp:name: Structural Layer
dp:layer_structural.dp:description: The immutable skeleton of the pedon. Contains everything that does not change between sensor readings. Populated from config dict, YAML, or SoilGrids fetch.

dp:layer_dynamic.dp:name: Dynamic Layer

dp:contents.dp:kv_store: Current live state per horizon (overwritten on each update)
dp:contents.dp:timeseries_log: Append-only history of all raw readings
dp:contents.dp:event_system: Five threshold alarms: saturation, wilting_point, high_temp, low_temp, high_ec

dp:layer_dynamic.dp:description: The live state machine. Every call to update() writes to the KV store, appends to the log, and checks all five threshold conditions.

dp:layer_functional.dp:name: Functional Layer
dp:layer_functional.dp:mutability: extensible — any solver can be injected at runtime

dp:built_in_solvers.dp:name: van_genuchten
dp:built_in_solvers.dp:reference: Van Genuchten, M.T. (1980). Soil Sci. Soc. Am. J., 44(5):892–898.

dp:built_in_solvers.dp:name: darcy_buckingham
dp:built_in_solvers.dp:description: Darcy-Buckingham vertical water flux between adjacent horizons
dp:built_in_solvers.dp:sign_convention: negative = downward drainage, positive = upward (waterlogging signal)

dp:built_in_solvers.dp:name: heat_equation
dp:built_in_solvers.dp:description: De Vries (1963) soil thermal properties model

dp:built_in_solvers.dp:name: carbon_flux_Q10
dp:built_in_solvers.dp:description: Q10 temperature sensitivity model for soil CO2 respiration

dp:layer_functional.dp:custom_solver_signature: def my_solver(params: dict, state: dict) -> dict
dp:layer_functional.dp:custom_solver_note: Any function matching the signature can be injected via register_method(). It receives the full structural params dict and full dynamic state dict, and must return a dict of new derived quantities.

dp:sensor_layer.dp:name: Sensor Layer (BYOD)
dp:sensor_layer.dp:description: Bring-Your-Own-Data input gateway. Users declare sensor fields in a manifest (YAML, JSON, or Python dict). The layer validates, converts units to SI, and renames all keys to canonical DP vocabulary before passing to the pedon.

dp:canonical_properties.dp:description: Volumetric soil water content — cm3 of water per cm3 of bulk soil
Soil matric potential — always negative; -15000 cm = permanent wilting point
Bulk soil electrical conductivity — proxy for salinity
Darcy-Buckingham vertical water flux — negative = downward drainage, positive = upward capillary rise or waterlogging
Soil CO2 respiration flux from organic matter decomposition
Normalised Difference Vegetation Index — user supplies extracted pixel value
Land surface temperature from thermal remote sensing
Remote sensing soil moisture index (e.g. from Sentinel-1 SAR)

dp:threshold_events.dp:description: Five alarm conditions checked on every update(). When crossed, all registered event handlers are called with (horizon_id, event_name, state_dict).

dp:public_api.dp:note: All methods accept and return plain Python dicts. No OOP knowledge required.

dp:methods.dp:name: build_pedon
dp:methods.dp:description: Build and return a DigitalPedon instance from a config dict.

dp:input.pedon_id: str — optional unique ID (auto-generated if absent)
dp:input.horizons: list[dict] — required, one dict per horizon

dp:methods.dp:name: update_sync
dp:methods.dp:description: Synchronous: ingest one normalised reading dict and run all solvers. Returns updated horizon state dict.

dp:input.horizon_id: str — must match a horizon in this pedon

dp:methods.dp:name: snapshot
dp:methods.dp:description: Return the full current state of every horizon as a nested dict.
dp:methods.dp:output: dict with pedon_id, site_name, latitude, longitude, timestamp, horizons[]

dp:methods.dp:name: query_history
dp:methods.dp:description: Return time-series history for one horizon as a list of dicts.
dp:methods.dp:output: list[dict] — each dict has timestamp, volumetric_water_content, soil_temperature_c, electrical_conductivity_ds_m

dp:methods.dp:name: register_method
dp:methods.dp:description: Inject a custom solver function into the framework. It runs on every update() after the built-in solvers.

dp:input.name: str — unique solver name
dp:input.fn: callable — def my_solver(params: dict, state: dict) -> dict
dp:input.inputs: list[str] — keys your solver reads from params/state

dp:methods.dp:name: register_event_handler
dp:methods.dp:description: Register a callback triggered when a threshold is crossed.
dp:methods.dp:input: callable — def handler(horizon_id: str, event_name: str, state: dict) -> None

dp:methods.dp:name: SensorLayer.ingest
dp:methods.dp:description: Ingest one raw reading dict. Validates, converts units, renames keys. Returns normalised dict ready for update_sync().
dp:methods.dp:input: dict — raw keys in any unit declared in the manifest
dp:methods.dp:output: dict — canonical keys, SI units, horizon_id, timestamp

dp:methods.dp:name: SensorLayer.split_by_horizon
dp:methods.dp:description: When one raw row contains fields from multiple horizons, split into one dict per horizon. Typical for multi-depth CSV loggers.
dp:methods.dp:output: list[dict] — one normalised dict per horizon_id

dp:methods.dp:name: SensorManifest.from_yaml
dp:methods.dp:description: Load a sensor manifest from a YAML file (requires PyYAML).

dp:methods.dp:name: register_soil_type
dp:methods.dp:description: Add a custom soil type to the global registry. It can then be referenced by name in any horizon config.

dp:methods.dp:name: to_json
dp:methods.dp:description: Serialise the current snapshot to a JSON string (GLOSIS-annotated).

dp:llm_usage_notes.dp:purpose: This manifest is designed to be injected as system context for any LLM assistant. After reading it, the LLM can: answer questions about the DP schema, generate correct Python code using the framework, interpret snapshot outputs, suggest appropriate threshold values, and call DP methods as tool calls (see dp_tools.json).
dp:workflow_summary: 1. Describe soil horizons in a config dict.
2. Call build_pedon(config) to create the digital twin.
3. Optionally declare sensor fields in a SensorManifest (YAML or dict).
4. Create a SensorLayer(manifest) to normalise incoming data.
5. For each sensor reading: call layer.ingest(raw) then pedon.update_sync(reading).
6. Call pedon.snapshot() to inspect current state.
7. Call pedon.query_history(horizon_id) to retrieve the time-series log.
8. Export to JSON-LD with pedon.to_json() for publication.

dp:common_patterns.dp:multi_horizon_csv: for row in csv.DictReader(f): [pedon.update_sync(r) for r in layer.split_by_horizon(layer.ingest_csv_row(row))]
